quality_notes: All 230 sessions contain exactly 90 one-minute reporting bars with no internal cadence defects.
The event source is separately hash-bound and is the sole source of strategy decisions and fills.
dataset.roll_policy: MotiveWave/Rithmic predeclared active-contract calendar; no back adjustment
transformations: Canonical one-minute bars retained only for dataset provenance and reporting context
Strategy decisions and fills use causally ordered Databento trade messages

ledger_queries: Clean-slate registry query on 2026-07-20 returned zero campaigns, variants, attempts, and runs.
Pre-reset campaigns and evidence were excluded because none had hash-bound manual mechanics approval.
duplicate_review.substantive_distinction: This is the first governed campaign after the user-authorized clean-slate reset; no archived, unverified result is treated as prior evidence or as an economic duplicate decision.

economic_edge_fingerprint.causal_mechanism: Passive liquidity absorbs one-sided aggressive trading near a known reference and price reverts toward fair value.
economic_edge_fingerprint.holding_period: Minutes to at most the 11:00 New York forced flatten; no overnight exposure.
economic_edge_fingerprint.market_behavior: Morning ES price repeatedly rejects a developing value-area boundary inside a balanced auction.
economic_edge_fingerprint.market_context: ES regular trading hours from 09:30 to 11:00 New York after a 16:00-to-09:30 overnight session.

draft.expected_mechanism: Repeated rejection at a developing value boundary suggests passive liquidity is absorbing aggressive flow; a fresh delta or large-trade event after the tap supplies causal confirmation for reversion.
draft.holding_horizon: Intraday only, from a post-tap stop entry between 09:30 and 10:59:59 New York until 11:00.
draft.hypothesis: During balanced ES morning auctions, a retest of a developing value-area edge that overlaps a prior liquidity reference and then attracts fresh one-sided trade activity may reverse toward fair value.
known_failure_modes: A developing profile can move while an order is pending, apparent one-sided delta can be continuation rather than absorption, and sparse trade prints can make a narrow AOI or local prominence unstable.
Trade-message aggressor classification, contract rolls, gap-through fills, and platform aggregation differences may cause mechanical entries to disagree with a MotiveWave/Rithmic chart.

sources.relevance: Primary source for the exact mechanics being forward tested and submitted for manual replay validation.
sources.title: Yush order-flow range-reversal rules and clarifications

sources.relevance: Documents clustering and predictive behavior around widely observed support and resistance levels.
sources.title: Support for Resistance: Technical Analysis and Intraday Exchange Rates

sources.relevance: Connects technical price levels with liquidity provision and limit-order-book behavior.
sources.title: Technical Analysis and Liquidity Provision

sources.relevance: Supports the general microstructure premise that signed order-flow imbalance has short-horizon price impact.
sources.title: The Price Impact of Order Book Events

draft.title: Yush Orderflow Range Reversal

variants.entry_rationale: A two-tick stop entry beyond the AOI demands observable movement away from the tested boundary, while neither the tap nor its post-tap trigger can be the event that created or materially changed the exact AOI; a tapped AOI is frozen until its developing-value anchor leaves the box.
known_failure_modes: Moving developing-value anchors can invalidate pending AOIs, and local delta prominence can be sensitive to neighboring buckets when activity is sparse or distributed across nearby prices.
Gap-through stop orders receive adverse event-price fills plus one tick, so actual risk may exceed the planned structural distance and cause otherwise attractive signals to be rejected.
variants.material_difference: As v01 this is the single frozen baseline mechanic; any later variant is prohibited until this implementation passes manual mechanics review and then receives a terminal FAIL in the test suite.
variants.mechanic_rationale: Build a causal AOI around developing VAL or VAH plus the strongest available market, local-delta, and large-trade categories; reset eligibility whenever the exact AOI price envelope or selected confluence identity changes, then require a prior reversal, a fresh directed tap, and a new post-tap trigger.
variants.stop_rationale: The stop sits two ticks beyond the opposite AOI edge but is widened to at least two points so tiny confluence envelopes do not create suffocating risk; entries above five points are rejected.
variants.target_rationale: The developing value midpoint is the first objective; after it trades, the stop protects 1.25 points and the opposite developing value edge becomes the final target, with an 11:00 hard flatten.
variants.timeframe_session_rationale: Trade messages are replayed in causal order from 09:30 to 11:00 New York; the three-minute delta bubble clock and 100 millisecond large-trade aggregation match the frozen chart interpretation.
variants.title: Exact forward-tested AOI and post-tap order-flow confirmation
